QF.id: XXVA Sales and Distribution of Research Services_24

question.text: Research analysts or research entities must disclose financial interest in the subject company. This includes disclosing whether:

answer.text: The analyst holds any educational qualifications related to the subject company's industry
The analyst/entity/associates have actual or beneficial ownership of 1% or more of the subject company's securities
The analyst has ever applied for a job at the subject company
The analyst follows the subject company on social media platforms

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI Regulation 19(i)(b) requires disclosure of whether the RA/entity/associates/relatives have actual/beneficial ownership of 1% or more of subject company securities at the end of the month preceding publication date. **The Trap:** The threshold is 1% — any ownership above this must be disclosed. Educational background, job applications, and social media follows are not material financial interests. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XV covers ownership disclosure requirements. The 1% threshold is also relevant in Series III-A for insider trading analysis. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Check the disclosure section of every research report before distributing — missing disclosures can indicate compliance lapses and expose both the entity and distribution personnel to regulatory risk.
